records.specific_details: STAG lobbied in connection with DLRCOCO's Draft Traveller Accommodation Programme 2025-2029 to ensure that enough Traveller-specific accommodation would be made available developed in Dun Laoghaire-Rathdown to meet the needs of Travellers living in the area and that Travellers' interests would be taken into account
records.intended_results: That DLRCOCO take into account Travellers' interests when adopting the Traveller Accommodation Programme 2025-2029

records.lobbyist_name: Dun Laoghaire Rathdown Chamber of Commerce
records.specific_details: Support placemaking through Future DLR
records.intended_results: Future DLR and Smart Dún Laoghaire are transforming how the county approaches urban development, using data driven insights to improve sustainability, accessibility, and community life. Budget 2026 should align with these initiatives by funding digital nfrastructure, smart mobility, and innovation-led placemaking that enhances public spaces and supports local businesses.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Public Expenditure, NDP Delivery and Reform

lobbying_activities: Town centres in Dún Laoghaire, Blackrock, and Dundrum hold untapped potential to repurpose vacant or underused commercial units—particularly above shops—into housing,coworking spaces, and community hubs. This will reduce vacancy, improve footfall, and foster more vibrant and inclusive urban cores. - Email

records.lobbyist_name: Dun Laoghaire Rathdown Chamber of Commerce
records.specific_details: Support delivery of DLR’s Climate Action Plan
records.intended_results: Dún Laoghaire–Rathdown’s Climate Action Plan setsambitious targets across energy efficiency, transportelectrification, biodiversity, flood resilience, and publicengagement. Budget 2026 should back its implementation through dedicated funding streams that enable local authorities to scale action quickly and visibly. Investment in practical community-led initiatives will help deliver on national targets while building public trust and participation.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Public Expenditure, NDP Delivery and Reform

lobbying_activities: DLR is already piloting effective low-carbon solutions, such as solar-powered street furniture and advanced retrofit schemes. With further support, these initiatives can be expanded across towns and neighbourhoods to lower emissions, reduce energypoverty, and support green jobs. Funding should prioritise retrofit grants, microgeneration infrastructure, and electrification of public transport and council fleets. - Lobby day

records.lobbyist_name: Dun Laoghaire Rathdown Chamber of Commerce
records.specific_details: Support delivery of DLR’s Climate Action Plan
records.intended_results: Dún Laoghaire–Rathdown’s Climate Action Plan setsambitious targets across energy efficiency, transportelectrification, biodiversity, flood resilience, and publicengagement. Budget 2026 should back its implementation through dedicated funding streams that enable local authorities to scale action quickly and visibly. Investment in practical community-led initiatives will help deliver on national targets while building public trust and participation.

records.lobbyist_name: Dun Laoghaire Rathdown Chamber of Commerce
records.specific_details: Unlocking Housing Supply in Growth Areas
records.intended_results: The Shanganagh Castle development in Shankill - 597 new homes delivered in partnership with the LDA and DLR County Council - sets a strong precedent for affordable, cost-rental, and social housing. Budget 2026 should build on this momentum by accelerating planning and funding for similar large-scale mixed-tenure projects across Dún Laoghaire–Rathdown, particularly in serviced land zones.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Public Expenditure, NDP Delivery and Reform

lobbying_activities: To meet urgent housing demand, interim modular housing should be deployed on council-owned landbanks while longterm builds progress. The opening of Woodbrook DART station on 10 August 2025 supports up to 2,300 new homes and must be matched by infrastructure investment and integrated housing delivery to unlock the full potential of the Shankill–Bray corridor. - Email

records.lobbyist_name: Dun Laoghaire Rathdown Chamber of Commerce
records.specific_details: Prioritize DLR SMEs in R&D tax credit expansions
records.intended_results: Many DLR-based SMEs are active in clean tech, biotech, and digital innovation but face barriers accessing R&D tax supports. Budget 2026 should simplify credit access for smaller firms, particularly in areas like Sandyford andCherrywood, where innovation clusters are growing. Thiswould boost local job creation and strengthen Ireland’snational research ecosystem.

records.lobbyist_name: Dun Laoghaire Rathdown Chamber of Commerce
records.specific_details: Support development of a Coastal Innovation District
records.intended_results: Dún Laoghaire–Rathdown is uniquely positioned to host Ireland’s first Coastal Innovation District, building on its access to the sea, harbour infrastructure, and nearby research institutions. Withstrong links to UCD, IADT, and Trinity, the area can drive innovation in blue economy sectors like offshore renewables and marine tech. Budget 2026 should support this vision through targeted investment and public-private partnership incentives.

records.specific_details: The lobbying intent was to raise awareness of our submission to the Department of Sport of our ask to make a Special Allocation in Budget 2026.  The ask was for €5m to fully renovate and restore a derelict building in Dun Laoghaire for use as a National Sailing Performance HQ.  This new building will replace the shipping containers which are currently in use, but which have no toilet, shower or changing facilities.
records.intended_results: The intended result was to raise awareness and understanding of our ask in order that the relevant ministers would support this in the pre-budget allocation

records.specific_details: The Wheel hosted launch event for our Pre-Budget Submission 2026. It comprised a panel discussion with speakers from community and voluntary organisations and an overview of The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026. We invited Ministers, TDs and Senators from the most relevant departments and joint Oireachtas committees and several attended in person.
records.intended_results: To inform TDs and Senators about The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026, which includes issues such as sustainable funding for the sector, equality of pay, increased compliance requirements, and the hidden infrastructure provided by the sector in areas such as health, social care, housing and climate action.

lobbying_activities: August 27th: Email to Ireland's 14 MEPs inviting them to an online briefing on the Indecon Report. - Email
July 10th:  The GPA's Pre-Budget Submission was emailed to Jack Chambers TD by GPA CEO Tom Parsons. - Submission
July 10th:  The GPA's Pre-Budget Submission was emailed to Patrick O'Donovan TD by GPA CEO Tom Parsons. - Submission

July 17th: Meeting with Fianna Fáil representatives facilitates by Paul McAuliffe TD, Fianna Fáil (Chair of Oireachtas Housing Committee) at Leinster House.  Other attendees included John Lahart TD and Cormac Devlin TD.  Other individuals came in and out of the meeting over the course of the hours.  Tom Parsons and Gemma Begley were joined at the meeting by Dublin players Brian Howard, Eve O’Brien, Eoin Murchan and Conor Burke.  Brendan Walsh from Blueyard also attended. - Meeting

July 2nd: Meeting with Seán Canney TD and Padraig Hughes at Leinster House.  This meeting was attended by Tom Parsons and Gemma Begley from the GPA.  they were joined by Galway hurler Conor Whelan and Mandy Johnston from Blueyard. - Meeting
June 11th: Email to Patrick Leonard at the Department of Transport requesting a meeting with Sean Canney TD from GPA CEO Tom Parsons. - Email
June 27th: Email to Alan Kelly TD to request an opportunity to present the findings of the Indecon Report to the Oireachtas Committee on Sport.  This email came from GPA CEO Tom Parsons. - Email
May 15th: Meeting with Minister Jack Chambers TD.  Tom Parsons attended the meeting on behalf of the GPA at the Department of Finance - Meeting

May 20th: Meeting with Minister Patrick O'Donovan TD, Theresa Newman and Ciaran Shanley at the Department of Sport. Tom Parsons and Gemma Begley attended this meeting on behalf of the GPA and were joined by GPA National Executive Committee member Neil McManus. - Meeting

records.specific_details: Seeking engagement with members of the Oireachtas to discuss the work of the Northern Ireland Assembly All-Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, and discuss gambling policy on the island of Ireland
records.intended_results: To secure support from members of the Oireachtas for the work of the Northern Ireland Assembly All-Party Group on Reducing Harm Related to Gambling, in particular to help draw attention to gambling marketing in sport in Ireland and the UK.

records.specific_details: Organisation of Front Line Defenders Award. Irish parliamentary Patrons of the Award include: Senator Malcolm Noonan, Senator Patricia Stephenson, Barry Ward TD, Cormac Devlin TD, Ivana Bacik TD, and Sean Crowe TD.
records.intended_results: Attendance at the FLD Awards as Patron for the State of Ireland, on May 22nd.
lobbying_activities: The annual Front Line Defenders Award for Human Rights Defenders at Risk was established in 2005 to honour the work of HRDs who are courageously making outstanding contributions to the promotion and protection of the human rights of others, often at great personal risk to themselves.Irish parliamentary Patrons of the Award 2025 include: Senator Malcolm Noonan, Senator Patricia Stephenson, Barry Ward TD, Cormac Devlin TD, Ivana Bacik TD, and Sean Crowe TD. - Event / Reception

records.intended_results: To make real time continuous glucose monitoring part of the standard model of care for diabetes patients in Ireland.

records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

records.lobbyist_name: Saint John of God Community Services Limited
records.specific_details: Trying to increase funding for housing and to resolve reduced respite and trying to gain access to CAS/CALF funding.
records.intended_results: To increase respite and residential options/ appropriately funded services.::The new policy on CAS Funding going in under Social Funding and lack of funds in the Co Meath & Co Louth budgets under CAS to purchase houses for people with Intellectual Disability. The fact now that people with Intellectual disability are not allowed to use CALF funding going forward however  CAS funding was made available to purchase a property in Co Meath
lobbying_activities: Emailed Minister O'Murchu's office - CALF/CAS - Email
Letters and emails were sent by families of those we support to the public officials and a group met with B Gloster - Letter
Phoned Minister O'Murchu's Office - CALF/CAS - Phone call

records.specific_details: Alcohol health information labelling
records.intended_results: Raise awareness of need for public knowledge of risks from alcohol including liver disease, cancer and FASD. No delay in the introduction of planned alcohol health information labelling
